hero.eyebrow: Open source · Lives in your menu bar
headline: Every link opens in the
right app
hero.sub: Junction becomes your default browser and routes every link you click — to the right browser, browser profile, or native app — by rules you write once. First match wins; anything unmatched opens in your fallback browser.

features.heading: Match on more than the domain.
features.lead: Rules are checked top to bottom and the first match wins. Every failure path — a missing browser, a deleted profile, an app that isn't installed — degrades to your fallback browser. A link is never lost.

cards.title: Wildcards, or full regex
cards.body: Match on host/path — *.atlassian.net/*, app.clickup.com/*. * stays inside a segment, ** crosses them, and *.example.com includes the apex domain. Drop to regex when the query string matters.

cards.title: Route by where the click came from
cards.body: A rule can match the app that opened the link — sourceApps: ["com.googlecode.iterm2"] sends Terminal links somewhere other than Mail links.

cards.title: Down to the browser profile
cards.body: Chromium profile directories (Default, Profile 1) and Firefox profile names. Work links land in the work profile, not just the work browser.

cards.title: Skip the tab entirely
cards.body: 17 built-in rewriters — Zoom, Slack, Figma, Notion, Spotify, Linear, Teams and more — turn a web URL into a native app link. Add your own from Settings → Deep Links.

cards.title: Decide at click time
cards.body: Hold ⌥ Option while clicking any link to force the picker, or give a rule a prompt action so it always asks.

cards.title: A config file you own
cards.body: Rules live in JSON at ~/.config/junction/config.json (honors $XDG_CONFIG_HOME). Edit in the GUI or your editor; it hot-reloads, and an invalid edit keeps the last-good rules.

matcher.lead: Rules are checked top to bottom, first match wins. Type a URL to see which rule catches it and where Junction would send it.

install.heading: Two ways to install.
install.homebrewNote:
The repo is its own tap, so point Homebrew straight at it — then trust the cask, since it lives outside Homebrew's official taps.
Homebrew also symlinks the bundled junction CLI onto your PATH.
install.requirements:
macOS 13 Ventura or later · Universal (Apple silicon + Intel)
Open the DMG and drag Junction into Applications.
install.quarantineNote: Junction isn't notarized by Apple yet, so macOS quarantines it and refuses the first launch — whichever way you install. Clear the flag afterward (or right-click Junction.app → Open → Open):
